item.title: 📤 Divestiture · CNDT
item.ns0:headline: Sells Transit Business to Modaxo for $164M cash; close expected by end of 2026
item.description:
Sells Transit Business to Modaxo for $164M cash; close expected by end of 2026

Agreed to sell its public transit and fare collection business to Modaxo for $164 million, subject to customary purchase price adjustments including cash, net tangible assets, and indebtedness. The transaction is expected to close before the end of 2026, pending regulatory approvals and other customary conditions. Conduent retains its tolling business and will use proceeds to fortify its balance sheet.

About: Conduent Incorporated provides business process services with capabilities in transaction-intensive processing, analytics, and automation in the United States, Europe, and internationally. It operates through three segments: Commercial Industries, Government Services, and Transportation. The Commercial Industries segment offers business process services and customized solutions to clients in various industries; and end-user customer experience management, transaction processing services, healthc…

  • Counterparty: Modaxo (Modaxo USA Holdings, Inc. and Modaxo France Holdings SAS)
  • Deal value: $164,000,000
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-21
  • Expected Close: before end of 2026

item.description:
Combining with NextEra Energy; close in 12-18 months

Announced a planned combination with NextEra Energy. The transaction is subject to federal and state regulatory approvals and shareholder votes of both companies. The companies expect the deal to close within 12 to 18 months.

About: Dominion Energy, Inc. produces and distributes energy in the United States. The company operates through four segments: Dominion Energy Virginia, Gas Distribution, Dominion Energy South Carolina, and Contracted Assets. The Dominion Energy Virginia segment generates, transmits, and distributes regulated electricity to approximately 2.7 million residential, commercial, industrial, and governmental customers in Virginia and North Carolina.

item.description:
Nominates 3-director slate at Vaxart; 2026 AGM

Concerned Vaxart Stockholders, led by Daniel P. Houle, are soliciting proxies to elect three nominees (Houle, Silverberg, Wallace) to the six-member board at the 2026 annual meeting. The group beneficially owns 1,515,343 shares and is using a gold universal proxy card. They urge stockholders to vote FOR their nominees and three unopposed company nominees, citing the board's disregard for shareholder votes on a reverse stock split.

The company's product pipeline includes norovirus vaccine, an oral tablet vaccine, which is in a Phase Ib clinical trial with bivalent oral tablet vaccine for the GI.1 and GII.4 norovirus strains; seasonal influenza vaccine, which is in Phase II clinical trial for the treatment of H1 influenza infection; respiratory sy…

item.ns0:headline: Vote set May 28 on CEO-led going-private merger
item.description:
Vote set May 28 on CEO-led going-private merger

Corporation is being acquired by an entity controlled by CEO Timothy Damadian in a going-private transaction. Stockholders will vote on the merger at a special meeting on May 28, 2026. This filing supplements the definitive proxy statement with additional disclosures regarding the background of the merger and financial projections, in response to stockholder litigation.

About: FONAR Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the research, development, production, and marketing of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanners for the detection and diagnosis of human diseases in the United States. The company operates through two segments, Medical Equipment segment, and Physician Management and Diagnostic services segment.

item.title: 🪙 Debt Restr. · SBIG
item.ns0:headline: Noteholders seize control of operating subsidiary; CEO removed
item.description:
Noteholders seize control of operating subsidiary; CEO removed

Received a notice from lead noteholders Shalcor Management and Lightbank II suspending all voting rights on its wholly-owned subsidiary SpringBig Inc., removing CEO Jaret Christopher, and appointing Coley Brown as interim CEO and Ivona Smith as director. The noteholders have not accelerated principal but the company has limited access to financial resources to continue operations. The company previously reported a default on its secured notes on April 28, 2026.

About: SpringBig Holdings, Inc. operates a software platform that provides customer loyalty and marketing automation solutions to cannabis retailers and brands in the United States and Canada. The company's platform connects consumers with retailers and brands through SMS marketing, emails, customer feedback system, and loyalty programs to support retailers and brands customer engagement and retention.

  • Counterparty: Shalcor Management / Lightbank II
  • Announced: 2026-05-15

item.title: 🤝 Merger · GRTX
item.ns0:headline: Merging with Obsidian; positive Phase 2 data (67% ORR) for OBX-115
item.description:
Merging with Obsidian; positive Phase 2 data (67% ORR) for OBX-115

Is merging with Obsidian Therapeutics in a reverse merger. Obsidian announced positive Phase 2 results for OBX-115 in advanced melanoma, showing 67% objective response rate (10/15) including 2 complete responses, with durable benefit and favorable safety. The merger remains pending shareholder vote and regulatory approvals; Obsidian plans to pursue accelerated FDA approval.

About: Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development and commercialization of therapeutics for the transformation of radiotherapy in cancer. The company's lead product candidate is avasopasem manganese (GC4419), a small molecule superoxide dismutase mimetic, which has completed Phase III clinical trial for the treatment of radiotherapy induced severe oral mucositis in patients with head and neck cancer (HNC); in Phase IIa clinical trial for the treatm…

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · PCRX
item.ns0:headline: Nominates director slate at Pacira; urges strategic review
item.description:
Nominates director slate at Pacira; urges strategic review

DOMA Perpetual Capital Management, owning 7.5% of Pacira BioSciences (PCRX), has launched a proxy contest to elect its slate of director nominees at the 2026 annual meeting. The activist criticizes the board for persistent underperformance and mismanagement of EXPAREL patent risk, and calls for a board-supervised process to explore strategic alternatives including a potential sale. The annual meeting date has not yet been announced.

About: Pacira BioSciences, Inc. provides non-opioid pain management and regenerative health solutions for healthcare practitioners and their patients in the United States. The company offers EXPAREL, a bupivacaine liposome injectable suspension; ZILRETTA, a triamcinolone acetonide extended-release injectable suspension; and iovera system, a non-opioid handheld cryoanalgesia device used to produce controlled doses of cold temperature only to targeted nerves.

  • Stake: 7.5

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · LULU
item.ns0:headline: Nominates director slate for 2026 AGM; seeks board change
item.description:
Nominates director slate for 2026 AGM; seeks board change

Dennis J. Wilson and affiliates filed a definitive proxy statement and GOLD universal proxy card to solicit votes for their slate of director candidates at lululemon athletica's 2026 annual meeting. The filing updates a prior Schedule 13D and details recent negotiations with the company, including support for eight key terms proposed by the issuer. Wilson expressed willingness to resolve through a vote or constructive settlement.

About: lululemon athletica inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, distributes, and retails athletic apparel and accessories for women and men. It operates in two segments, Company-Operated Stores and Direct to Consumer. The company offers pants, shorts, tops, and jackets for healthy lifestyle and athletic activities, such as yoga, running, and training, as well as other sweaty pursuits. It also provides fitness-related accessories and footwear.

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · KTB
item.ns0:headline: Sells Lee subsidiary to Authentic Brands Group for $750M cash + $250M earnout
item.description:
Sells Lee subsidiary to Authentic Brands Group for $750M cash + $250M earnout

Agreed to sell its wholly-owned Lee subsidiary to Authentic Brands Group for $750 million in cash at closing, plus up to $250 million in earnout based on Lee's performance over five years. The transaction was unanimously approved by Kontoor's board and is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary conditions. Proceeds will be used for debt reduction and potential share repurchases.

About: Kontoor Brands, Inc., a lifestyle apparel company, designs, manufactures, procures, markets, and distributes denim, apparel, and accessories under the Wrangler, Lee, and Rock & Republic brands in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Wrangler and Lee. The company sells its products primarily through mass merchants, specialty stores, mid-tier and traditional department stores, company-operated stores, and online.

  • Consideration: cash

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · ECAT
item.ns0:headline: Glass Lewis recommends voting for all board nominees vs Saba slate
item.description:
Glass Lewis recommends voting for all board nominees vs Saba slate

Faces a proxy contest from Saba Capital, which has nominated a full slate of directors. Glass Lewis, a leading proxy advisory firm, recommended that shareholders vote for all nine of ECAT's board nominees on the white card, rejecting Saba's slate. The annual meeting is scheduled for June 9, 2026. Glass Lewis noted ECAT's discount near 2.8% and distribution rate as best-in-class, and that Saba has sold down nearly a third of its stake.

About: BlackRock ESG Capital Allocation Term Trust is a newly-organized, non-diversified, closed-end management investment company with no operating history. It provides total return and income through a combination of current income, current gains and long-term capital appreciation. The company is headquartered in Wilmington, DE.

  • Counterparty: Saba Capital

item.description:

GameStop Corp. proposed to acquire eBay Inc. for $125 per share in a combination of cash and GameStop common stock. The non-binding proposal was delivered to eBay's board on May 3, 2026; GameStop holds economic exposure to ~29.1M shares via put/call options. The company's Marketplace platform includes its online marketplace at ebay.com and the eBay suite of mobile apps. item.description:
Acquires Critical Prospecting for $1.197M in shares; 1:5.75 consolidation

Linear Minerals entered a letter of intent to acquire Critical Prospecting Corp., owner of the Mooney Lake Uranium, Hatsoff Tungsten-Molybdenum, and Gorge Lithium projects, for 4,788,000 post-consolidation shares at $0.25/share, totaling $1.197M. Concurrently, the company plans a 5.75:1 share consolidation and non-brokered private placements of up to $2.5M (flow-through and hard dollar units). The transaction is subject to definitive agreements, due diligence, and CSE approval.

About: Linear Minerals Corp., a junior resource company, engages in the exploration and development of mineral properties in Canada. It primarily explores for lithium deposits. The company's flagship property is the Augustus Lithium property that includes 21 mineral claims covering an area of approximately 900 hectares located in the Abitibi area of Western Quebec. The company was formerly known as FE Battery Metals Corp. and changed its name to Linear Minerals Corp. in December 2024.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · CLBK
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Northfield Bancorp for $14.25/sh cash or 1.425x stock; vote June 25
item.description:
Acquires Northfield Bancorp for $14.25/sh cash or 1.425x stock; vote June 25

Is acquiring Northfield Bancorp (NFBK) in a cash/stock election merger concurrent with its conversion from mutual to stock holding company. NFBK shareholders can elect $14.25/sh cash or 1.425x shares of the new Columbia Financial, Inc. (expected to trade under CLBK), with adjustments if appraised value exceeds $2.3B or $2.6B. Votes at both companies' meetings on June 25, 2026.

About: Columbia Financial, Inc., a bank holding company, provides financial services to businesses and consumers in the United States. The company offers non-interest-bearing demand deposits, such as individual and commercial checking accounts; interest bearing demand accounts comprising interest earning checking accounts and municipal accounts; and savings and club accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit.

  • Counterparty: Northfield Bancorp, Inc. (NFBK)
  • Offer price: $14.25

item.ns0:headline: Sells Global Knowledge Training to EHJob GP for up to $18M; close Q2
item.description:
Sells Global Knowledge Training to EHJob GP for up to $18M; close Q2

Entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Global Knowledge Training business (instructor-led training) to EHJob GP LLC for total consideration of up to $18 million, consisting of $10 million initial cash at closing (subject to working capital adjustments) plus $10 million deferred consideration less approximately $2 million for long-term employee liabilities, payable in five quarterly installments. The initial payment may be funded by a seller note from the business to Skillsoft. Skillsoft intends to use net proceeds for general corporate purposes and to focus on its core AI-native skills management platform. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including Saudi Arabia competition clearance, and is expected to close in the second fiscal quarter (by July 31, 2026).

About: Skillsoft Corp. provides corporate digital learning services in the United States and internationally. The company operates through Content, Global Knowledge, and SumTotal segments. It offers enterprise learning solutions to prepare organizations for the future of work, as well as enable them to overcome critical skill gaps, drive demonstrable behavior-change, and unlock the potential in their greatest assets.

item.ns0:headline: Won NMPA approval for fruquintinib/sintilimab combo in RCC
item.description:
Won NMPA approval for fruquintinib/sintilimab combo in RCC

HUTCHMED and Innovent announced NMPA approval for ELUNATE (fruquintinib) plus TYVYT (sintilimab) for second-line advanced/metastatic renal cell carcinoma. The FRUSICA-2 study showed a 63% reduction in risk of progression or death (HR 0.373) and median PFS of 22.2 months vs 6.9 months for axitinib/everolimus. This is the 10th approved indication for sintilimab.

About: HUTCHMED (China) Limited discovers, develops, and commercializes targeted therapeutics and immunotherapies for cancer and immunological diseases in HongKong and internationally. It operates in Oncology/Immunology and Other Ventures segments. The company develops Savolitinib, an inhibitor for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), papillary and renal cell carcinoma, colorectal cancer (CRC), and gastric cancer (GC); and Fruquintinib, an inhibitor for CRC, breast cancer, GC, endometrial cancer (EMC), …

The trustee has not participated in negotiations; unitholder approval would be required.

item.ns0:headline: Board files revocation statement to oppose Neugebauer's second consent solicitation for June 30 special meeting
item.description:

Board filed a preliminary Consent Revocation Statement opposing former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a special meeting on or about June 30, 2026, to replace the board with his nominees and force a sale. The board cites Neugebauer's termination for cause, an 80% stock decline during his tenure, and that his proposals would force a sale at a depressed valuation. Neugebauer and affiliates control approximately 40% of shares; the board has amended bylaws to require 70% approval for board changes.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · GPRO
item.ns0:headline: Wins patent case; $8.2M award vacated; all claims invalid
item.description:
Wins patent case; $8.2M award vacated; all claims invalid

The U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California granted GoPro's motion for judgment as a matter of law, invalidating the sole remaining patent claim (claim 11 of U.S. Patent No. 8,890,954) as obvious. The court vacated the $8.2 million damages award from the October 2025 jury verdict and denied all of Contour IP Holding's post-trial motions. GoPro has no liability from the case, though the ruling is subject to appeal to the Federal Circuit.

About: GoPro, Inc. engages in manufacturing and selling cameras and camera accessories. It provides mountable and wearable cameras and accessories, which it refers to as capture devices. Its product brands include HERO9 Black, HERO8 Black, Max, HERO7 Black, HERO7 Silver, GoPro Plus, and GoPro App. The company was founded by Nicholas Woodman in 2002 and is headquartered in San Mateo, CA.

  • Counterparty: Contour IP Holding LLC
  • Announced: 2026-05-14

item.ns0:headline: IRG contributes $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% stake; $2.00/share implied value; vote pending
item.description:
IRG contributes $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% stake; $2.00/share implied value; vote pending

Entered into a Contribution Agreement with Industrial Realty Group Global to contribute IRG's industrial real estate portfolio (98 assets, ~$2.9B gross asset value) in exchange for ~94.1% of the combined company's OP units and Class B shares with 51% voting power. Sachem existing shareholders will retain ~5.9% ownership; the transaction implies a $2.00 per share value for Sachem common stock, a 90% premium to the 30-day VWAP. The deal is subject to Sachem shareholder approval, regulatory conditions, and pre-closing reorganization including a 20-to-1 reverse stock split, redomestication to Delaware, and name change to IRG Realty Trust; closing expected by end of 2026.

About: Sachem Capital Corp. operates as a real estate finance company. The company is involved in the originating, underwriting, funding, servicing, and managing a portfolio of short-term loans secured by first mortgage liens on real property located primarily in Northeastern United States and Florida. It offers loans to real estate investors and owners to fund their acquisition, renovation, rehabilitation, development, and/or improvement of residential or commercial properties.

  • Counterparty: Industrial Realty Group Global

  • Deal value: ~$3.4B implied combined enterprise value
  • Premium: 90%
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-17
  • Expected Close: end of 2026

item.description:

item.description:

item.description:

The Radoff-JEC Group, owning ~7.8% of Seer, submitted a third non-binding proposal to acquire the company for $2.40 per share in cash plus a contingent value right (CVR) entitling stockholders to 80% of net proceeds from any sale of Seer's assets. About: Seer, Inc., a life sciences company, engages in developing and commercializing products to decode the secrets of the proteome. Entered a Third Amended and Restated Convertible Promissory Note with Aljomaih Automotive Co., reducing the conversion price from $71.451 to $12.00 per share and adding a mandatory conversion feature if VWAP exceeds $16 for 20 of 30 trading days. The $15.5M outstanding principal is now convertible into 1,291,666 shares, and Aljomaih holds 28.0% beneficial ownership. Eos will raise up to $150M via a rights offering to fund its Class B contribution. Closing requires DOE consent, shareholder approval of authorized share increase, and execution of commercial framework.

About: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. designs, manufactures, and deploys battery storage solutions for utility, commercial and industrial, and renewable energy markets in the United States. It offers stationary battery storage solutions. The company's flagship product is the Eos Znyth DC battery system designed to meet the requirements of the grid-scale energy storage market. Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. was founded in 2008 and is headquartered in Edison, New Jersey.

item.description:
Saba proxy contest for ECAT board; vote pending

Saba Capital is waging a proxy contest for board seats at BlackRock ESG Capital Allocation Term Trust (ECAT). Saba has been actively selling its ECAT position while seeking board representation. item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · MLTX
item.ns0:headline: FDA aligns on BLA submission for sonelokimab in HS; submission end-Sept 2026
item.description:
FDA aligns on BLA submission for sonelokimab in HS; submission end-Sept 2026

Announced positive outcome from final pre-BLA meeting with FDA for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). FDA aligned on submission plans and label strategy, including acceptability of MIRA trial data for effectiveness and inclusion of adolescent data from VELA-TEEN. BLA submission planned for end of September 2026; acceptance and Priority Review decision expected by end of November 2026; first commercial launch targeted for H2 2027 if approved. Company also reported Q1 2026 cash of $357.9M.

About: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, engages in developing therapies. It is developing Sonelokimab, a novel investigational Nanobody therapy for the treatment of inflammation. The company is involved in conducting Phase II trials for hidradenitis suppurativa, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, or radiographic axial spondyloarthritis. MoonLake Immunotherapeutics was founded in 2021 and is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland.

  • Announced: 2026-05-10

The company updated its estimated range of additional liquidating distributions, based on 88.9 million common shares outstanding, with sales expected to complete by mid-2026.

Backed by decades of experience, expertise and ambition, we create value by transforming insights into strategy and strategy into action. As of October 29, 2020, the Company's portfolio of 45 properties includes approximately 3.7 million square feet of commercial space and 6,863 multifamily apartment units.

item.description:
Files Form 10 for ADI spin-off; Surran named Resideo CEO

Filed a Form 10 registration statement for the spin-off of its ADI global distribution business, which will operate as ADI Global Distribution Inc. The filing also announces board and management changes effective upon completion of the separation, including the appointment of Thomas Surran as President and CEO of Resideo post-spin. The spin-off remains pending, with no specific closing date disclosed.

item.title: 🔐 Going Private · KOBELCO WIRE
item.ns0:headline: Kobe Steel to take private via share exchange; vote June 26, delist Aug 28
item.description:
Kobe Steel to take private via share exchange; vote June 26, delist Aug 28

Kobe Steel, Ltd. announced a share exchange to acquire all outstanding shares of Kobelco Wire Company, Ltd., making it a wholly owned subsidiary. The transaction is subject to approval at Kobelco Wire's annual shareholders meeting on June 26, 2026, and is expected to close on September 1, 2026. Kobelco Wire's shares will be delisted from the Tokyo Stock Exchange on August 28, 2026.

  • Counterparty: Kobe Steel, Ltd.
